verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I transmembrane protein whose primary localization is the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment and cis-Golgi network. Although it has a single transmembrane domain, its large luminal domain (GOLD) and N-terminal architecture are oriented toward the ER/Golgi lumen, not the extracellular space. The protein functions as a cargo receptor for intracellular trafficking (cargo recognition, COPII/COPI assembly, GPI-anchored protein export). Its role is entirely within the secretory pathway interior. No documented evidence of stable plasma membrane surface localization or therapeutic targeting via surface-directed modalities. The protein is endomembrane-resident and does not reach the outer face of the plasma membrane under baseline conditions.
